content:
**Seattle Seahawks Capture Super Bowl LX: A New Era Begins**

February 8, 2026 — LAS VEGAS

The Seattle Seahawks triumphed in Super Bowl LX on Sunday night, defeating the Kansas City Chiefs 31-27 in a breathtaking finale at Allegiant Stadium. With this victory, the Seahawks clinched their second Lombardi Trophy, ending a twelve-year championship drought and igniting celebrations across the Pacific Northwest.

Seattle’s meteoric rise this season culminated in a performance for the ages. Quarterback Jalen McCarthy, the 2025 first-round pick, was named Super Bowl MVP after throwing for 312 yards and three touchdowns, including the game-winning strike to D.K. Metcalf with just 48 seconds remaining. “This team never stopped believing,” McCarthy said, hoisting the trophy amid a shower of blue and green confetti. “We fought for every yard, every play. Seattle, this is for you!”

Head coach Pete Carroll, who returned from retirement last spring, credited the team’s resilience. “There’s something special about this group,” Carroll beamed. “They’ve written their own story, and tonight, they made Seahawks history.”

The Seahawks’ defense sealed the win as rookie cornerback Malik Carter intercepted Patrick Mahomes’ final pass attempt, sending Seattle fans into a frenzy both in the stadium and back home. The city’s iconic Space Needle was illuminated in Seahawks colors as jubilant fans poured into the streets to celebrate a season—and a night—the city will never forget.
